vulnerability.hypothesis: In IsObjectEscaped (line 287-304), the MSlots case only asserts in DEBUG builds that MSlots consumers are StoreDynamicSlot/LoadDynamicSlot. In release builds, if an MSlots node somehow has a consumer that is NOT one of those (e.g., through optimization passes creating unexpected uses), the object would be incorrectly considered non-escaped. The scalar replacement would then proceed to eliminate the object allocation, but the unexpected consumer would operate on invalid/missing memory. This is especially dangerous because the assertion at line 292 (MOZ_ASSERT(ins->object() != 0)) appears to be checking a pointer value against 0, not an operand index — it should likely be checking that the object is the correct operand.
